items.title: Evaluation and Societal Discussion of Emotion Fidelity of AI-generated Human Avatars
path: Evaluation and Societal Discussion of Emotion Fidelity of AI-generated Human Avatars

items.title: Abstract
items.text: This paper evaluates the emotional resonance and fidelity of video generated by state-of-the-art Generative AI (GenAI) models. Through a structured focus group session with film industry professionals, we assess the performance of Wan2.1, Hunyuan-Video, and Runway models across text-to-video and audio-driven generation tasks. While current generative architectures achieve high visual quality, we find significant limitations in their capacity to express subtle, complex human emotions. We examine these constraints, mapping the evolution of model architectures and detailing the specific emotional expression deficits observed during user trials, highlighting key areas for future research in responsible and creative AI.
path: Evaluation and Societal Discussion of Emotion Fidelity of AI-generated Human Avatars

items.title: Experiment Videos
items.text: Compare the emotional expressions generated by Runway and Wan2.1 against the original actor references and input images across different categories:
path: Evaluation and Societal Discussion of Emotion Fidelity of AI-generated Human Avatars

items.title: Anger Expression
items.text: Actor Reference (Original) GenAI Input (Reference) Model Video Expert #1 Comments Expert #2 Comments Runway High visual quality and resolution, but the character has a relatively static face with a blank stare, lacking emotional micro-expressions. The anger feels superficial; the character's facial muscles do not contract realistically to match high-intensity rage. Wan2.1 Face warping and distortion under high-intensity emotion. The jaw and mouth area show artifacting. The animation breaks suspension of disbelief due to temporal inconsistencies and facial stretching.
path: Evaluation and Societal Discussion of Emotion Fidelity of AI-generated Human Avatars

items.title: Joy Expression
items.text: Actor Reference (Original) GenAI Input (Reference) Model Video Expert #1 Comments Expert #2 Comments Runway High aesthetic appeal, but the smile feels fake or "plastic", failing to engage the eyes (Duchenne smile deficit). Looks like a stock photo animated; lacks the warm, natural transitions of genuine joy. Wan2.1 Struggles to keep facial structure stable while smiling, leading to melting or warping. Poor temporal coherence around the teeth and lips.
path: Evaluation and Societal Discussion of Emotion Fidelity of AI-generated Human Avatars

items.title: Encouragement / Motivation
items.text: Actor Reference (Original) GenAI Input (Reference) Model Video Expert #1 Comments Expert #2 Comments Runway Steady head pose, but the eyes remain dead and unresponsive. The motivational expression is flat and robotic, lacking the nuance of a trained actor. Wan2.1 Accurate lip-syncing but fails to connect vocal intensity with upper facial expressions. Distortions occur in the cheek area during larger head movements.
path: Evaluation and Societal Discussion of Emotion Fidelity of AI-generated Human Avatars

items.title: Grateful Expression
items.text: Actor Reference (Original) GenAI Input (Reference) Model Video Expert #1 Comments Expert #2 Comments Runway The emotional tone is lost, resulting in a neutral or blank look rather than gratitude. The character doesn't show the micro-expressions of vulnerability or emotion. Wan2.1 Cross-modal mismatch; voice is trembling and emotional, but the eyes are completely vacant. The model cannot animate subtle eye movements (like welling up) to match the audio.
path: Evaluation and Societal Discussion of Emotion Fidelity of AI-generated Human Avatars
